text: Hello everyone, a quick surgical operation to connect the white bag to the left white after we lost the right white in a good condition. We found this bag on the left white, we put it in a plastic bag so that even if anything happens to the contents inside the bag, it is found in the cornea. As you can see now, the white with the white bag inside this plastic bag. Then we will start searching for the place where we will cut through it so that we can separate it from the white. The white, of course, is this outer white color. As you can see here,

The surgeon employs a technique to separate the outer layer of the cystic structure from its inner contents without cutting into the bag itself, minimizing the risk of rupture. The outer white tissue is incised to facilitate peeling off the cystic structure, which appears sticky in the upper part.

The surgeon smoothly cuts the remaining attached part of the transparent coating, preserving the white parts. The surgical field is washed and cleaned, and a single cut is made on the white paper for even shaping before removing the plastic bag.
